channel.title: Opportunities for Youth
channel.description: Empowering Youth through Access to Information and Opportunities

item.title: F.J. Brotherton Charitable Trust Grant 2026: Complete Guide to Funding Opportunities for Nonprofits in the United States
item.dc:creator: Maria Ramirez
item.description:
The F.J. Brotherton Charitable Trust is now inviting applications from qualified nonprofit organizations seeking funding support for impactful community initiatives in 2026. This grant program offers structured application cycles throughout the year, giving eligible organizations multiple opportunities to apply for financial assistance to support charitable projects within approved U.S. regions. With clearly defined submission windows, […]

The post F.J. Brotherton Charitable Trust Grant 2026: Complete Guide to Funding Opportunities for Nonprofits in the United States appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The F.J. Brotherton Charitable Trust is now inviting applications from qualified nonprofit organizations seeking funding support for impactful community initiatives in 2026. This grant program offers structured application cycles throughout the year, giving eligible organizations multiple opportunities to apply for financial assistance to support charitable projects within approved U.S. regions.

With clearly defined submission windows, eligibility requirements, and funding timelines, this opportunity is designed to support 501(c)(3) organizations committed to meaningful social impact.

Grant Application Cycles and Key Deadlines

The Trust operates two annual application cycles:

Cycle 1: March – June

  • Letter of Intent (LOI) Window: March 1 – May 31
  • Full Application Deadline: June 30
  • Funding Distribution: August 15 – September 1

Cycle 2: September – December

  • Letter of Intent (LOI) Window: September 1 – November 30
  • Full Application Deadline: December 31
  • Funding Distribution: February 15 – March 1

All applications must be submitted online through the official grant portal. Late submissions or incomplete applications will not be considered.

Application Process Overview

The application process consists of three main steps:

1. Registration

Organizations must register on the Trust’s online portal. Returning applicants are already registered and can proceed using existing credentials.

2. Letter of Intent (LOI)

Applicants submit a brief proposal outlining their project. The Trust reviews submissions within 2–3 weeks to determine eligibility for the next stage.

3. Full Grant Application

If the LOI is accepted, applicants complete a full application before the cycle deadline.

Final funding decisions are typically made approximately two months after each application deadline during Advisory Board meetings held in February and August.

Important Funding Restrictions

Applicants must carefully review eligibility criteria before applying:

  • Grants are limited to organizations operating in the United States
  • Primary funding regions include:
    • Florida
    • Massachusetts
    • New Jersey
    • New York
    • South Carolina
  • Organizations must hold valid 501(c)(3) tax-exempt status
  • Funding is not provided for:
    • Legal aid programs
    • Loans or debt financing
    • Moving expenses
    • Public policy or advocacy groups
    • Surveys or research studies
    • Grants to individuals
  • International organizations and overseas project funding are not eligible
  • Prior grantees must wait 18 months before reapplying

Application Guidelines and Requirements

  • Applications are accepted only online
  • Email or mailed submissions are not permitted
  • All documents must use alpha-numeric file names only
  • Contact information must remain up to date throughout the process
  • Progress reports are required every six months after funding approval
  • The full process may take up to six months from application to final decision

Why This Grant Matters

This funding opportunity is ideal for nonprofits seeking:

  • Sustainable funding for community development projects
  • Support for educational, health, and social programs
  • Long-term partnership opportunities with a charitable trust
  • Structured, recurring grant cycles for annual planning

Early application is strongly recommended, as late submissions may not allow sufficient time for completion of the review process.

How to Apply

Organizations can begin by registering on the official F.J. Brotherton Charitable Trust grant portal and submitting a Letter of Intent during the active application window. For assistance, applicants may contact: kelly@fjbrothertoncharitabletrust.org

APPLY HERE. 

For more information about this apportunity, click here.

Discover more grants opportunities, click here.

Conclusion

The F.J. Brotherton Charitable Trust grant program provides a valuable funding pathway for eligible nonprofit organizations across select U.S. states. With two annual cycles, a structured review process, and significant funding potential, this opportunity supports mission-driven organizations committed to long-term community impact.

The post F.J. Brotherton Charitable Trust Grant 2026: Complete Guide to Funding Opportunities for Nonprofits in the United States appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: International Opportunities at IDOS MGG Academy 2026: Internship & Tutor Roles in Global Cooperation
item.dc:creator: Hadir Arfaoui
item.description:
The German Institute of Development and Sustainability (IDOS) is opening doors for ambitious students and young professionals through two distinct opportunities under its prestigious Managing Global Governance (MGG) Academy 2026. Hosted in Bonn, this programme is a gateway into the world of international cooperation, diplomacy, and global policy-making.  Opportunity 1: Internship in International Cooperation & […]

The post International Opportunities at IDOS MGG Academy 2026: Internship & Tutor Roles in Global Cooperation appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The German Institute of Development and Sustainability (IDOS) is opening doors for ambitious students and young professionals through two distinct opportunities under its prestigious Managing Global Governance (MGG) Academy 2026. Hosted in Bonn, this programme is a gateway into the world of international cooperation, diplomacy, and global policy-making.

 Opportunity 1: Internship in International Cooperation & Event Management

 Overview

IDOS is seeking a highly motivated intern to support the MGG Academy team for a 3-month full-time internship starting on 1 September 2026. This role focuses on programme coordination, participant support, and communication activities.

The MGG Academy itself runs from 11 September to 29 November 2026, bringing together emerging leaders from across the globe for training, dialogue, and collaboration. The programme includes study trips, notably to Brussels, offering direct exposure to institutions like the European Union.

 Key Responsibilities

As an intern, you will be actively involved in:

  • Supporting international participants throughout their stay in Bonn
  • Coordinating arrivals, departures, and logistics for study trips
  • Assisting in organizing and implementing training sessions
  • Contributing to social media and communication strategies
  • Producing and editing visual content (photo/video using DSLR)
  • Preparing reports, training materials, and Excel analyses
  • Supporting administrative and project management tasks

 Candidate Profile

Ideal candidates should:

  • Be enrolled in a Bachelor’s or Master’s degree (any field)
  • Have initial experience in event management or communications
  • Demonstrate interest in international relations and global cooperation
  • Possess strong German and English language skills
  • Be comfortable with MS Office and digital collaboration tools
  • Show strong organizational, creative, and intercultural skills
  • Be willing to work on-site in Bonn

 Benefits

  • Work in a dynamic, international environment
  • Gain exposure to global institutions such as the United Nations and EU
  • Participate in a study trip to Brussels
  • Receive a monthly stipend of €450
  • Develop practical skills in event management and international cooperation

 Important Information

  • Bachelor’s students: only mandatory internships allowed
  • Master’s students: mandatory or voluntary internships accepted
  • Applicants must confirm:
    • Internship type
    • Availability (starting 1 September 2026)
    • Willingness to be based in Bonn

APPLY HERE 

 Opportunity 2: MGG Academy Tutor Role (Student Assistant / Freelancer)

 Overview

IDOS is also recruiting a Tutor to support participants throughout the MGG Academy programme. This role is more flexible and focuses on participant assistance, logistics, and well-being.

The tutor acts as a key contact point for participants, ensuring their smooth integration into life in Bonn.

 Key Responsibilities

The tutor will:

  • Welcome participants upon arrival and assist with accommodation
  • Provide orientation support (city navigation, SIM cards, etc.)
  • Assist with administrative procedures (registration, bank accounts)
  • Offer weekly office hours at IDOS
  • Support participants with daily life tasks (appointments, logistics)
  • Act as an emergency contact (including evenings/weekends)
  • Assist with departure and closing logistics

 Candidate Profile

Applicants should:

  • Be enrolled students or self-employed (freelancer under small business regulation)
  • Have excellent German and English skills (C1–C2 level)
  • Possess strong intercultural and communication skills
  • Be familiar with Bonn and its infrastructure
  • Have experience supporting groups or communities
  • Demonstrate flexibility and strong organizational abilities
  • Show interest in international relations and development cooperation

 Compensation

  • €14/hour (Bachelor students)
  • €15/hour (Master students)

Working hours depend on participant needs and programme activities.

 Application Process

Applicants should send:

  • CV
  • Cover letter
  • Relevant certificates

APPLY HERE 

 Why You Should Apply

Both opportunities provide a rare chance to engage directly with a globally recognized programme in international cooperation. Whether you choose the structured internship or the dynamic tutor role, you will:

  • Build strong international experience
  • Expand your professional network
  • Gain insight into global governance systems
  • Work in a multicultural, high-impact environment

For students and young professionals aiming for careers in diplomacy, international organizations, or development cooperation, the MGG Academy at IDOS offers a powerful stepping stone.

Discover more opportunities here

The post International Opportunities at IDOS MGG Academy 2026: Internship & Tutor Roles in Global Cooperation appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: FAO Internship Programme 2026 in Rome, Italy (Paid UN Internship for Students and Graduates)
item.dc:creator: Hilda Gyenti
item.description:
The Food and Agriculture Organization of the United Nations (FAO) is inviting applications for its 2026 Internship Programme at FAO Headquarters (HQ) in Rome, Italy. This opportunity is designed for motivated students and recent graduates who are eager to gain hands-on experience while contributing to global efforts to end hunger and improve food security. About […]

The post FAO Internship Programme 2026 in Rome, Italy (Paid UN Internship for Students and Graduates) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Food and Agriculture Organization of the United Nations (FAO) is inviting applications for its 2026 Internship Programme at FAO Headquarters (HQ) in Rome, Italy. This opportunity is designed for motivated students and recent graduates who are eager to gain hands-on experience while contributing to global efforts to end hunger and improve food security.

About FAO

FAO is a specialized agency of the United Nations leading international efforts to defeat hunger and ensure food security for all. With 195 member countries and operations in over 130 countries, FAO works toward building sustainable and resilient agri-food systems worldwide.

 Internship Overview

  • Programme: FAO Internship Programme (HQ)
  • Location: Rome, Italy
  • Duration: 3 to 11 months
  • Application Deadline: 26 August 2026
  • Job Type: Internship
  • Who Can Apply: Students and recent graduates

The internship offers participants the chance to apply academic knowledge in a real-world setting while contributing to FAO’s mission and the UN Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs).

Programme Objectives

The FAO Internship Programme aims to:

  • Attract talented young individuals with innovative ideas
  • Provide practical learning experiences in FAO-related fields
  • Support FAO’s strategic goals for sustainable agriculture and food systems
  • Contribute to the 2030 Agenda for Sustainable Development

Interns will work under the supervision of FAO professionals and contribute to ongoing projects aligned with their academic background.

 Roles and Responsibilities

Interns will:

  • Support projects and tasks defined in their Terms of Reference (TOR)
  • Work in various technical areas depending on their expertise
  • Collaborate with multicultural teams
  • Contribute to research, analysis, and operational activities

Eligibility Criteria

To qualify, applicants must:

  • Be enrolled in an undergraduate or graduate programme or be a recent graduate
  • Study in a field relevant to FAO’s work
  • Be aged 21 to 30 years
  • Be a national of an FAO member country
  • Have working knowledge of at least one FAO language (Arabic, Chinese, English, French, Russian, or Spanish)
  • Demonstrate strong communication skills and adaptability in a multicultural environment

Note: Academic qualifications must be from institutions recognized by UNESCO/IAU (WHED database).

 FAO Core Competencies

Successful candidates are expected to demonstrate:

  • Results focus
  • Teamwork
  • Effective communication
  • Relationship building
  • Knowledge sharing and continuous learning

 Benefits

  • Monthly stipend (if not sponsored by another institution)
  • Hands-on international work experience
  • Exposure to global development and UN systems
  • Opportunity to contribute to impactful global initiatives

Diversity & Inclusion

FAO strongly encourages applications from:

  • Women
  • Nationals of underrepresented member countries
  • Persons with disabilities
  • Candidates from developing regions including LDCs, LLDCs, and SIDS

FAO is committed to maintaining a diverse and inclusive workforce.

Important Notes

  • FAO has a zero-tolerance policy for harassment, discrimination, and abuse
  • No application or recruitment fees are required at any stage
  • Candidates who previously completed an FAO internship are not eligible
  • Applicants must have valid residence or immigration status for Italy before starting
  • Applications must be complete to be considered

How to Apply

To apply:

  1. Visit the FAO Careers Portal (Jobs at FAO)
  2. Create or update your online profile
  3. Upload required documents:
    • Motivation letter
    • Proof of enrollment or academic qualifications
  4. Submit your application through the portal

Only applications submitted via the official FAO recruitment system will be considered.

 Application Deadline

26 August 2026 — Apply early to increase your chances.

Pro Tip

Tailor your motivation letter to highlight how your academic background aligns with FAO’s mission and demonstrate your interest in global food security and sustainable development.

This internship is a strong stepping stone for anyone looking to build a career in international development, agriculture, or global policy within the United Nations system.

Discover similar Internship opportunities Here.

The post FAO Internship Programme 2026 in Rome, Italy (Paid UN Internship for Students and Graduates) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Reuters News Accelerator Program 2026: Global Opportunity for Mid-Career Journalists (Apply Now)
item.dc:creator: Hilda Gyenti
item.description:
The Reuters News Accelerator Program (RNAP) is back for its second year, offering an exciting opportunity for mid-career journalists ready to step into the global spotlight. Designed by Reuters, one of the world’s most trusted news organizations, this intensive program aims to nurture exceptional talent and shape the next generation of international reporters and editors. […]

The post Reuters News Accelerator Program 2026: Global Opportunity for Mid-Career Journalists (Apply Now) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Reuters News Accelerator Program (RNAP) is back for its second year, offering an exciting opportunity for mid-career journalists ready to step into the global spotlight. Designed by Reuters, one of the world’s most trusted news organizations, this intensive program aims to nurture exceptional talent and shape the next generation of international reporters and editors.

Following a successful launch in Africa, the 2026 edition expands into China and Taiwan, reinforcing Reuters’ commitment to strengthening global journalism from the ground up.

About the Reuters News Accelerator Program (RNAP)

RNAP is a highly competitive initiative tailored for journalists with 5–8 years of professional experience. It combines hands-on newsroom experience, expert mentorship, and advanced training to help participants thrive in today’s fast-evolving media landscape.

Participants will work alongside seasoned Reuters journalists, contributing to impactful stories that influence global conversations.

Key Benefits of the Program

1. Cutting-Edge Journalism Skills

Gain expertise in multimedia storytelling, data journalism, and digital news production, equipping you with tools essential for modern reporting.

2. In-Depth Global Knowledge

Develop a strong understanding of major global issues, particularly in politics, finance, and business.

3. Expert Mentorship

Receive personalized coaching from experienced Reuters editors and industry leaders to refine your reporting and storytelling skills.

4. Global Exposure

Collaborate within an international newsroom and build connections with a diverse network of journalists worldwide.

5. Career Advancement Opportunities

Position yourself for leadership roles in global journalism and make a meaningful impact in the media industry.

Available Roles in 2026

Reuters is recruiting talented journalists for several key positions across China and Taiwan, including:

  • China Consumer & Retail Correspondent – Report on consumer trends, spending patterns, and retail markets.
  • China Financial Services Correspondent – Cover regulatory developments and financial policy in one of the world’s largest economies.
  • China Manufacturing Correspondent – Lead reporting on manufacturing, robotics, and industrial technology.
  • China Political & General News Correspondent – Cover politics, policy shifts, and global geopolitical implications.
  • Senior Photographer – Deliver compelling visual storytelling on breaking and enterprise news.
  • Senior Video Journalist – Produce high-impact video content and mentor visual journalism teams.

What Makes RNAP Unique?

A Culture of Excellence

Work with one of the most respected news organizations committed to high-quality, accurate, and impactful journalism.

Global Perspective

Gain firsthand experience in regions that play a critical role in shaping the global economy and politics.

Real-World Impact

Contribute to stories that matter and help shape public understanding of key global issues.

Who Should Apply?

This program is ideal for journalists who:

  • Have 5–8 years of reporting experience
  • Demonstrate strong storytelling and analytical skills
  • Are eager to work in a fast-paced, global newsroom
  • Want to grow into leadership roles in journalism

How to Apply

Interested candidates can explore specific roles and apply through the official Reuters careers platform. Each role includes detailed requirements and application instructions.

The Reuters News Accelerator Program 2026 is more than just a career opportunity—it’s a gateway to becoming a globally recognized journalist. With world-class mentorship, hands-on experience, and exposure to major international stories, RNAP provides the tools and platform to take your journalism career to the next level.

If you’re ready to move beyond local reporting and make a global impact, this program is worth serious consideration.

Discover similar opportunities Here.

The post Reuters News Accelerator Program 2026: Global Opportunity for Mid-Career Journalists (Apply Now) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Fully Funded African Research Universities Alliance Early Career Research Fellowship 2026–2027: Prestigious Pan-African Opportunity for Young Researchers to Advance Global Academic Impact
item.dc:creator: Isaac O
item.description:
The African Research Universities Alliance Early Career Research Fellowship stands out as one of the most competitive and impactful research opportunities available to young African scholars today. Designed to strengthen Africa’s research ecosystem and elevate globally competitive scholarship, this fellowship provides early-career academics with the resources, mentorship, and international exposure needed to thrive. Backed by […]

The post Fully Funded African Research Universities Alliance Early Career Research Fellowship 2026–2027: Prestigious Pan-African Opportunity for Young Researchers to Advance Global Academic Impact appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The African Research Universities Alliance Early Career Research Fellowship stands out as one of the most competitive and impactful research opportunities available to young African scholars today. Designed to strengthen Africa’s research ecosystem and elevate globally competitive scholarship, this fellowship provides early-career academics with the resources, mentorship, and international exposure needed to thrive.

Backed by globally recognized partners such as the Mastercard Foundation and the Carnegie Corporation of New York, the program is a major investment in the future of African research and innovation.

This is not just a fellowship—it is a career-defining opportunity that connects scholars to elite research centers, fosters interdisciplinary collaboration, and accelerates academic excellence across the African continent.

Fellowship Overview: What You Need to Know

The ARUA Early Career Research Fellowship is a fully funded, six-month residential research program hosted at top African universities affiliated with ARUA’s Centres of Excellence and Africa-Europe Clusters of Research Excellence.

Key Highlights:

  • Duration: 6 months
  • Fellowship Period Options:
    • March 2026 – August 2026
    • September 2026 – February 2027
  • Number of Fellowships: Up to 42 positions
  • Funding Includes:
    • Monthly stipend of USD 2,000
    • Accommodation support
    • Return travel costs
  • Gender Priority: At least 70% of positions reserved for women

Who Can Apply? (Eligible Countries and Regions)

This fellowship is specifically targeted at:

  • African nationals from all countries across Africa
  • Applicants may be based at:
    • ARUA member universities
    • Non-ARUA universities within Africa

This makes the opportunity broadly accessible across the continent, including East Africa, West Africa, Southern Africa, Central Africa, and North Africa.

Target Audience: Who Qualifies for This Fellowship?

The program is tailored for:

  • Early-career researchers
  • Young academic staff (lecturers, postdoctoral researchers)
  • Scholars seeking international research collaboration
  • Individuals aiming to build a strong global academic profile

Eligibility Criteria: Detailed Requirements

To qualify, applicants must meet strict academic and professional standards:

Academic Qualifications:

  • Must hold a PhD degree in a relevant discipline
  • Must be within 5 years of completing their PhD

Age Requirement:

  • Must be 35 years old or younger at the time of application

Professional Criteria:

  • Must have a strong research track record
  • Should be actively working on a relevant research project
  • Must demonstrate potential for international academic impact

Additional Requirements:

  • Must be available to spend 6 months away from home institution
  • Must provide proof of institutional support
  • Must have accessible research data or materials for the fellowship period

Special Considerations:

  • Female applicants are strongly encouraged to apply
  • Limited opportunities exist for female postdoctoral fellows

Fields of Research and Areas of Focus

The fellowship spans a wide range of disciplines through ARUA’s Centres of Excellence and Clusters:

Natural Sciences:

  • Climate and Development
  • Energy and Water Systems
  • Non-Communicable Diseases
  • Sustainable Food Systems

Social Sciences and Humanities:

  • Governance and Inequality
  • Migration and Mobility
  • Urbanization
  • Identity and Post-Conflict Societies

Emerging and Interdisciplinary Fields:

  • Artificial Intelligence and Data Science
  • Public Health and Genomics
  • Renewable Energy
  • Peace and Conflict Studies

This diversity ensures that applicants from nearly all academic disciplines can find a relevant research niche.

Responsibilities of Selected Fellows

Successful candidates are expected to actively contribute to research excellence through:

  • Conducting collaborative research projects
  • Publishing at least one high-impact academic paper
  • Delivering presentations and participating in workshops
  • Supporting research dissemination and partnerships

These responsibilities are designed to ensure measurable academic output and long-term career growth.

Application Requirements: Documents You Must Prepare

Applicants must submit a comprehensive application package, including:

  • Cover letter detailing research goals
  • Full academic CV
  • Institutional support letter
  • Proof of PhD qualification
  • Research concept note (up to 1,500 words)
  • Two academic writing samples
  • Copy of passport biodata page

A strong application requires clarity, relevance, and alignment with ARUA’s research priorities.

Apply here

Selection Process: How Candidates Are Chosen

The selection process is highly competitive and involves:

  • Evaluation by Centre Directors and academic leaders
  • Assessment based on:
    • Academic excellence
    • Research productivity
    • Relevance of proposed research
    • Alignment with host institution priorities

Only the most outstanding candidates will be selected.

Why This Fellowship Matters for Your Career

Participating in the ARUA Early Career Research Fellowship offers:

  • Access to world-class research infrastructure
  • Collaboration with leading African and European scholars
  • Enhanced publication and research output
  • Strengthened academic networks and career trajectory

This fellowship is a strategic stepping stone for researchers aiming to become global leaders in academia.

Deadline

15th July 2026

The ARUA Early Career Research Fellowship is more than just funding—it is a platform for transformation. It empowers young African researchers to push the boundaries of knowledge, engage in impactful research, and contribute meaningfully to global academic discourse.

For more information visit here

For more global opportunities click here

The post Fully Funded African Research Universities Alliance Early Career Research Fellowship 2026–2027: Prestigious Pan-African Opportunity for Young Researchers to Advance Global Academic Impact appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Apply Now: Fully Funded PhD Studentships 2026 in the UK – Birmingham Newman University
item.dc:creator: Hilda Gyenti
item.description:
Birmingham Newman University Graduate School has announced an exciting opportunity for prospective researchers with the launch of two fully funded PhD studentships for 2026. These studentships are set to commence in October 2026 and are aimed at supporting outstanding candidates pursuing doctoral research in the UK. Overview of the PhD Studentships Institution: Birmingham Newman University […]

The post Apply Now: Fully Funded PhD Studentships 2026 in the UK – Birmingham Newman University appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Birmingham Newman University Graduate School has announced an exciting opportunity for prospective researchers with the launch of two fully funded PhD studentships for 2026. These studentships are set to commence in October 2026 and are aimed at supporting outstanding candidates pursuing doctoral research in the UK.

Overview of the PhD Studentships

  • Institution: Birmingham Newman University
  • Programme: PhD Studentships 2026
  • Location: Birmingham, United Kingdom
  • Funding Type: Fully Funded (for UK/Home students only)
  • Stipend: £21,805 per year
  • Duration: 3 years (full-time)
  • Application Deadline: 31st May 2026
  • Start Date: October 2026

Funding and Benefits

Successful applicants will receive funding aligned with UKRI studentship rates, which includes:

  • Annual stipend of £21,805 (2026/27 rate)
  • Full coverage of PhD tuition/registration fees
  • Financial support for the entire 3-year duration of the programme

This funding package ensures that candidates can fully focus on their research without financial burden.

Eligibility Criteria

To be considered for the studentships, applicants must:

  • Be classified as a UK/Home student
  • Hold one of the following academic qualifications:
    • An MPhil, or
    • A Master’s/MRes degree with Distinction, or
    • A Master’s/MRes degree with Merit, plus a Distinction in the dissertation component

Research Areas

Applications are open across all research areas supported by Birmingham Newman University, provided there is:

  • Suitable academic supervision
  • Available capacity within the chosen research field

Applicants are encouraged to explore the university’s research strengths and supervisory expertise before applying.

Selection Process

  • Applications will be reviewed by an interview panel
  • Final recommendations will depend on:
    • Interview performance
    • Acceptance for PhD registration by both:
      • Birmingham Newman University
      • Liverpool Hope University (degree-awarding partner)

How to Apply

Interested candidates must follow the official application procedure, which includes:

  1. Completing an Expression of Interest form
  2. Clearly indicating ‘Studentship’ as the funding source in the application
  3. Submitting the form through the official university application portal

Applicants should ensure they carefully follow all instructions provided on the university’s website.

Important Dates

  • Application Deadline: 31st May 2026 (23:59)
  • Interview Date: 15th June 2026
  • Programme Start: October 2026

This is a competitive and prestigious opportunity for aspiring researchers seeking fully funded doctoral study in the UK. With comprehensive financial support and access to quality supervision, Birmingham Newman University provides a strong foundation for academic and professional growth.

Early preparation and a well-developed research proposal will significantly strengthen your application.

Discover similar opportunities Here.

The post Apply Now: Fully Funded PhD Studentships 2026 in the UK – Birmingham Newman University appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Apply Now: Dana Foundation Grants 2026 Offering Up to €150,000 for Grassroots NGOs
item.dc:creator: Hilda Gyenti
item.description:
Applications are now open for the Dana Foundation Grants Programme 2026, offering up to €150,000 in flexible funding to support locally led organizations tackling multidimensional poverty and driving sustainable community impact. This initiative is specifically designed to empower grassroots and community-based organizations with long-term, unrestricted funding, enabling them to scale solutions and respond effectively to […]

The post Apply Now: Dana Foundation Grants 2026 Offering Up to €150,000 for Grassroots NGOs appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Applications are now open for the Dana Foundation Grants Programme 2026, offering up to €150,000 in flexible funding to support locally led organizations tackling multidimensional poverty and driving sustainable community impact.

This initiative is specifically designed to empower grassroots and community-based organizations with long-term, unrestricted funding, enabling them to scale solutions and respond effectively to evolving local needs.

About the Dana Foundation Grants Programme

The Dana Foundation Grants Programme focuses on supporting locally led, evidence-based initiatives that address interconnected social and economic challenges.

Built on a trust-based philanthropy model, the programme emphasizes:

  • Flexibility in fund usage
  • Reduced administrative burden
  • Long-term partnerships with grantees

Rather than funding short-term projects, the programme supports organizations holistically, strengthening their capacity to create lasting, systemic change within their communities.

Funding Details

Selected organizations will receive:

  • Up to €150,000 over 3 years
  • Approximately €50,000 per year
  • Unrestricted funding (flexible use)
  • Simplified reporting requirements
  • Ongoing long-term partnership support

This funding approach is designed to strengthen organizations as a whole—not just individual projects.

Focus Areas

The programme supports initiatives addressing multidimensional poverty, including:

  • Education access and quality
  • Health and well-being
  • Livelihoods and economic empowerment
  • Social inclusion and equality
  • Climate resilience

Additionally, the programme encourages:

  • Systems-thinking approaches
  • Community-led solutions
  • Evidence-based programming
  • Cross-sector interventions

Eligibility Criteria

To qualify, applicants must:

  • Be locally led, community-based organizations
  • Have an annual budget under €500,000
  • Operate in regions with high levels of multidimensional poverty
  • Demonstrate strong governance and financial management systems
  • Have a clear strategy or theory of change
  • Show evidence of impact and learning systems

Priority is given to organizations deeply rooted in the communities they serve.

Programme Features

Key highlights include:

  • Multi-year funding (3 years)
  • Flexible, unrestricted grants
  • Reduced reporting requirements
  • Strong focus on organizational strengthening
  • Emphasis on long-term systemic impact

This structure allows organizations to focus more on delivering impact and less on administrative processes.

Why You Should Apply

Unlike many traditional grants, the Dana Foundation Grants Programme offers flexible, trust-based funding, allowing organizations to allocate resources where they are most needed.

For grassroots organizations working on long-term development challenges, this programme provides the stability, autonomy, and support needed to scale impact sustainably and strengthen communities from within.

Application Deadline

Deadline: December 31, 2026

Applicants are strongly encouraged to apply early to allow sufficient time for preparation and submission.

How to Apply (Step-by-Step)

1. Visit the Application Portal

Start your application here

2. Check Eligibility

Confirm your organization meets all criteria, including budget size and local leadership requirements.

3. Prepare Your Organizational Profile

Outline your mission, governance structure, and community impact.

4. Define Your Strategy

Present your theory of change and long-term vision.

5. Demonstrate Impact

Provide evidence of your past work, measurable outcomes, and learning systems.

6. Submit Your Application

Carefully review all details before final submission.

Get more information about this grant, by visiting HERE

Discover similar grant opportunities Here.

The post Apply Now: Dana Foundation Grants 2026 Offering Up to €150,000 for Grassroots NGOs appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: 2027 Global Investment Banking Summer Analyst Program (Equity Capital Markets) – Apply Now | $110,000 Salary | New York
item.dc:creator: admin
item.description:
Job Information Job Identification210736238 Job CategoryAnalysts Business UnitCommercial & Investment Bank Posting Date04/20/2026, 05:00 AM Locations New York, NY, United States Apply Before05/02/2026, 04:55 AM Job ScheduleFull time Base Pay/SalaryNew York,NY $110,000.00-$110,000.00 Job Description Spend your summer working alongside industry-leading professionals who contribute to high-profile transactions and drive growth within a leading global investment bank. Our […]

The post 2027 Global Investment Banking Summer Analyst Program (Equity Capital Markets) – Apply Now | $110,000 Salary | New York appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Job Information

  • Job Identification210736238
  • Job CategoryAnalysts
  • Business UnitCommercial & Investment Bank
  • Posting Date04/20/2026, 05:00 AM
  • Locations New York, NY, United States
  • Apply Before05/02/2026, 04:55 AM
  • Job ScheduleFull time
  • Base Pay/SalaryNew York,NY $110,000.00-$110,000.00

Job Description

Spend your summer working alongside industry-leading professionals who contribute to high-profile transactions and drive growth within a leading global investment bank. Our industry and product teams provide sound advice, facilitate access to capital, and make vital connections, all while helping our clients achieve their goals.

 

About the Program

As a Summer Analyst in the Market Intelligence team in Equity Capital Markets, you’ll help promote innovation for our clients. In this nine-week program, you’ll receive comprehensive training, hands-on learning opportunities, continuous mentoring and feedback, and development opportunities through meaningful projects that challenge you and build your skills.

 

Joining us means you’ll become part of our inclusive team and culture, unified by the firm’s mission and commitment to success. Based on individual performance, those who successfully complete the program may receive offers to return full time the following year.

 

You can learn more about our Equity Capital Markets and Market Intelligence here.

 

Job Responsibilities: 

  • Combine real-time market intelligence with investor views to provide actionable insight on equity capital markets origination and execution
  • Develop client-facing presentations and thought leadership that provide differentiated advice and insights to corporate, sponsor, and investor clients
  • Support investor relationship development, including planning and executing bespoke touchpoints and events
  • Participate in client transactions – including all equity capital markets deals – working closely with senior leaders from initial pitch through to closing
  • Learn how we support the growth of our clients and communities
  • Develop innovative solutions to address complex, real-world business challenges
  • Strengthen your technical skills in corporate finance

 

Required Qualifications, Capabilities and Skills:

  • Fluency in English
    • Fluency means the ability to easily read, write, speak, and comprehend spoken English
  • Expected graduation date between December 2027 and June 2028 from a bachelor’s or master’s program
    • Master’s candidates must complete their degree within two years of receiving a bachelor’s degree
  • Minimum cumulative GPA of 3.4 on a 4.0 scale
  • Authorization to work in the U.S.
    • To be eligible for this program, you must be authorized to work in the U.S. We do not offer any type of employment-based immigration sponsorship for this program. Likewise, JPMorganChase, will not provide any assistance or sign any documentation in support of any other form of immigration sponsorship or benefit except optional practical training (OPT) or curricular practical training (CPT.) 

 

Preferred Qualifications, Skills and Capabilities:

  • Demonstrated energy and commitment to building a career in equity capital markets
  • Strong analytical, accounting, finance, quantitative, and financial modeling skills
  • Intermediate proficiency in Microsoft Office (Excel, PowerPoint, Outlook, etc.)
  • Excellent communication skills and ability to interact effectively with senior professionals, clients, and key stakeholders
  • Self-directed, highly motivated, and able to work independently as well as part of a dynamic team
  • Adaptability, flexibility, and resilience in fast-paced environments

 

We fill our classes on a rolling basis and strongly encourage you to apply as early as possible before job postings close.

About You

A strong interest in investment banking is essential, as are analytical and quantitative abilities. Flexibility to thrive in a dynamic, collaborative environment is also important. Attention to detail, resilience under pressure, and a well-rounded academic background are highly valued. And while a degree in Finance, Business or Economics is not required, a solid level of numeracy is essential.

 

Beyond academics, we value the unique qualities, interests, and achievements that make you who you are. Your initiative, creativity, and perspective are what set you apart and contribute to our team’s success.

 

About Us

At JPMorganChase, we’re creating positive change for the diverse communities we serve. We do this by championing your innovative ideas through a supportive culture that helps you every step of the way as you build your career. If you’re passionate, curious and ready to make an impact, we’re looking for you.

 

What’s next?

Help us learn about you by submitting a complete and thoughtful application, which includes your resume. Your application and resume are ways for us to initially get to know you, so it’s important to complete all relevant application questions so we have as much information about you as possible.  

 

After you confirm your application, we will review it to determine whether you meet certain required qualifications.

 

If you are advanced to the next step of the process, you’ll receive an email invitation to complete a video interview, powered by HireVue within 48 hours. This is your opportunity to further bring your resume to life and showcase your experience for our recruiting team and hiring managers.

 

You must complete the HireVue video interview within 7 days for your application to be considered further.

 

JPMorgan Chase is committed to creating an inclusive work environment that respects all people for their unique skills, backgrounds and professional experiences. We strive to hire qualified, diverse candidates, and we will provide reasonable accommodations for known disabilities.

 

Visit jpmorganchase.com/careers for upcoming events, career advice, our locations and more.

 

APPLY NOW 

About Us

JPMorganChase, one of the oldest financial institutions, offers innovative financial solutions to millions of consumers, small businesses and many of the world’s most prominent corporate, institutional and government clients under the J.P. Morgan and Chase brands. Our history spans over 200 years and today we are a leader in investment banking, consumer and small business banking, commercial banking, financial transaction processing and asset management.

 

We offer a competitive total rewards package including base salary determined based on the role, experience, skill set and location. Those in eligible roles may receive commission-based pay and/or discretionary incentive compensation, paid in the form of cash and/or forfeitable equity, awarded in recognition of individual achievements and contributions. We also offer a range of benefits and programs to meet employee needs, based on eligibility. These benefits include comprehensive health care coverage, on-site health and wellness centers, a retirement savings plan, backup childcare, tuition reimbursement, mental health support, financial coaching and more. Additional details about total compensation and benefits will be provided during the hiring process.

We recognize that our people are our strength and the diverse talents they bring to our global workforce are directly linked to our success. We are an equal opportunity employer and place a high value on diversity and inclusion at our company. We do not discriminate on the basis of any protected attribute, including race, religion, color, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expression, age, marital or veteran status, pregnancy or disability, or any other basis protected under applicable law. We also make reasonable accommodations for applicants’ and employees’ religious practices and beliefs, as well as mental health or physical disability needs. Visit our FAQs for more information about requesting an accommodation.

JPMorgan Chase & Co. is an Equal Opportunity Employer, including Disability/Veterans

About the Team

J.P. Morgan’s Global Banking business is one of the largest wholesale banking client franchises in the world. We serve clients, including corporations, governments, states, municipalities, healthcare organizations, education institutions, banks and investors.

Global Investment Banking supports a broad range of corporations, institutions and governments by providing strategic advice, capital raising and risk management expertise.

APPLY NOW 

The post 2027 Global Investment Banking Summer Analyst Program (Equity Capital Markets) – Apply Now | $110,000 Salary | New York appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Nordic Climate Adaptation Programme 2026: Major Funding Opportunity for Collaborative Climate &Biodiversity Projects
item.dc:creator: Mercy N. Atsuki
item.description:
The Nordic Climate Adaptation Programme (NCAP) has officially opened its 2026 call for project funding, inviting organizations, researchers, and institutions to submit proposals for collaborative projects focused on climate adaptation across the Nordic region. This initiative is part of the broader Programme for Nordic Co-operation on the Environment and Climate 2025–2027, designed to strengthen resilience, […]

The post Nordic Climate Adaptation Programme 2026: Major Funding Opportunity for Collaborative Climate &Biodiversity Projects appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Nordic Climate Adaptation Programme (NCAP) has officially opened its 2026 call for project funding, inviting organizations, researchers, and institutions to submit proposals for collaborative projects focused on climate adaptation across the Nordic region. This initiative is part of the broader Programme for Nordic Co-operation on the Environment and Climate 2025–2027, designed to strengthen resilience, sustainability, and cross-border cooperation.

With substantial funding available and a strong emphasis on innovation, policy impact, and practical implementation, this opportunity is ideal for stakeholders looking to influence climate adaptation strategies and biodiversity protection at a regional level.

Overview of the Funding Opportunity

The NCAP call aims to support strategic, high-impact projects that address shared climate challenges across Nordic countries. The program focuses on:

  • Strengthening collaboration between Nordic nations
  • Promoting knowledge exchange and innovation
  • Supporting sustainable investments and biodiversity protection
  • Delivering tangible benefits to governments, private sector actors, and citizens

Funding Details

  • Grant size: 450,000 – 800,000 DKK per project
  • Total funding pool: 3,000,000 DKK
  • Number of projects funded: Approximately 2–3 projects per theme

Key Focus Areas (Themes)

Applicants are invited to submit proposals under one of two major themes:

Theme A: Climate Adaptation Investments & Risk Reduction

This theme focuses on strengthening the role of the private sector in climate adaptation by improving how climate risks are understood, assessed, and financed.

Core Objectives

  • Increase private sector investment in climate adaptation
  • Enhance understanding of climate-related risks in investment decisions
  • Develop policies and incentives that encourage adaptation financing

Areas of Interest

Projects may address one or more of the following:

  1. Linking Adaptation Investments to Risk Reduction
    • Quantifying how climate adaptation reduces physical and financial risks
    • Developing innovative risk-sharing instruments
  2. Integration of Climate Risks in Investment Decisions
    • Identifying barriers and opportunities in incorporating climate risks into investment frameworks
  3. Strengthening Incentives and Policy Frameworks
    • Designing regulatory and market-based mechanisms to encourage investment
    • Supporting collective adaptation efforts where private returns are limited
  4. Financing Models and Instruments
    • Exploring public-private partnerships and blended finance models
    • Scaling climate adaptation investments
  5. Information and Decision-Support Tools
    • Improving access to climate risk data
    • Developing tools to guide investment decisions

Sector Focus

Projects are encouraged to include sector-specific perspectives, especially in high-risk sectors such as:

  • Construction and infrastructure
  • Energy systems
  • Urban development

Theme B: Climate Change Impact on Biodiversity and Ecosystems

This theme focuses on understanding and mitigating the effects of climate change on ecosystems and biodiversity in the Nordic region.

Core Objectives

  • Analyze climate impacts on ecosystems
  • Identify knowledge gaps and research needs
  • Support development of effective mitigation strategies

Areas of Interest

Projects may address:

  1. Impact on Nordic Ecosystems
    • Effects of long-term climate change and extreme events (e.g., droughts, wildfires)
    • Links between ecosystem degradation and societal risks
  2. Monitoring Systems
    • Evaluation of current monitoring methods and indicators
    • Identification of gaps in monitoring systems
  3. Availability of Quantitative Data
    • Assessment of existing biodiversity and ecosystem data
    • Identification of key data gaps
  4. Mitigation Options and Their Effectiveness
    • Evaluation of conservation strategies (e.g., protected areas, land management practices)
    • Exploring synergies between different mitigation approaches
  5. Climate Refugia
    • Identifying areas that can preserve biodiversity under climate stress
    • Enhancing ecosystem resilience across the region

Practical Application Requirement

Projects must clearly demonstrate how their findings will:

  • Be communicated effectively
  • Be translated into actionable strategies for policymakers and authorities

Eligibility and Collaboration Requirements

  • Projects must involve at least three Nordic countries:
    • Sweden, Denmark, Norway, Iceland, Finland, Faroe Islands, Greenland, or Åland
  • One Nordic country may be replaced by a non-Nordic partner
  • Projects must demonstrate strong collaboration and shared regional value

Application Process

To apply, applicants must submit:

  1. Completed Application Form (Word or PDF format)
  2. Budget and Financial Statements (Excel format)
  3. De minimis declaration (if applicable)

Submission Method

  • All documents must be submitted via email in a single application package
  • Email: ncap@naturvardsverket.se
  • For more information about this opportunity, click HERE

Support Contact

For inquiries, applicants can contact the NCAP Secretariat directly via the same email.

Important Dates

  • Application Opening Date: 8 April 2026
  • Application Deadline: 24 May 2026 (23:59 CET)
  • Notification of Results: June 2026

Project Timeline

  • Latest completion date: 30 November 2027
  • Preferred completion of activities: Before 30 September 2027
  • Final deadline for activities: 31 October 2027

Why This Opportunity Matters

This funding call represents a significant opportunity to:

  • Influence climate adaptation policy in the Nordic region
  • Drive innovation in climate finance and biodiversity protection
  • Strengthen cross-border collaboration
  • Contribute to long-term environmental sustainability

Projects funded under this program are expected to deliver both strategic insights and practical solutions, making a real impact on climate resilience and ecosystem preservation.

Discover more Grant opportunities on OFY HERE

The post Nordic Climate Adaptation Programme 2026: Major Funding Opportunity for Collaborative Climate &Biodiversity Projects appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Catalytic Grant Fund 2026/2027: Unlocking Innovation in Pathogen Surveillance for Global Health
item.dc:creator: Diakaridia KONE
item.description:
The Catalytic Grant Fund Application Cycle 2026/2027, launched by the World Health Organization (WHO) through the International Pathogen Surveillance Network (IPSN), represents a powerful funding opportunity for organizations committed to strengthening global health systems. This initiative is designed to accelerate innovation in pathogen surveillance, particularly in low- and middle-income countries (LMICs), where early detection and […]

The post Catalytic Grant Fund 2026/2027: Unlocking Innovation in Pathogen Surveillance for Global Health appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Catalytic Grant Fund Application Cycle 2026/2027, launched by the World Health Organization (WHO) through the International Pathogen Surveillance Network (IPSN), represents a powerful funding opportunity for organizations committed to strengthening global health systems. This initiative is designed to accelerate innovation in pathogen surveillance, particularly in low- and middle-income countries (LMICs), where early detection and response systems are most critical.

With funding ranging from $50,000 to $250,000, this grant supports impactful, short-term projects that generate evidence, promote research, and contribute to scalable solutions in disease surveillance.

What is the Catalytic Grant Fund?

The Catalytic Grant Fund is a strategic initiative hosted by the United Nations Foundation on behalf of IPSN. It aims to:

  • Support innovative pilot projects in pathogen surveillance
  • Strengthen genomic and environmental monitoring systems
  • Build evidence for scaling up successful interventions
  • Promote knowledge sharing and global collaboration

The fund is backed by leading global philanthropic organizations, including:

  • Bill & Melinda Gates Foundation
  • Rockefeller Foundation
  • Wellcome Trust
  • Institute of Philanthropy

Key Focus Areas (Technical Tracks)

Applicants must submit proposals under one of the following two tracks:

  1. Pathogen Genomic Surveillance
    • Leveraging genomic technologies to detect and monitor pathogens
    • Enhancing sequencing capacity and data analysis
  2. Wastewater and Environmental Surveillance
    • Monitoring pathogens through environmental samples
    • Supporting early warning systems for outbreaks

Funding Details

  • Grant Amount:$50,000 – $250,000
  • Project Duration:Up to 12 months
  • Scope:Pilot initiatives aligned with IPSN priorities
  • Outcome Focus:Research, innovation, and scalable impact

Who Can Apply?

The fund is open to:

  • IPSN member organizations in LMICs
  • Consortia applications, where the lead applicant is an IPSN member from an LMIC
  • Non-member organizations, provided they apply for IPSN membership alongside their proposal

The IPSN strongly encourages applications from:

  • Early-career researchers
  • Diverse backgrounds, regardless of gender, ethnicity, religion, disability, or sexual orientation

Application Process (Step-by-Step)

The application process is structured in two stages:

Stage 1: Concept Note Submission

  • Submit a short concept note aligned with the Request for Proposals (RfP)
  • Reviewed by IPSN Secretariat and independent experts

Stage 2: Full Proposal (Shortlisted Applicants Only)

  • Selected applicants submit detailed proposals
  • Evaluation includes technical review and possible clarifications
  • Final decisions communicated after assessment
  • Successful applicants undergo due diligence by the UN Foundation

Important Rule: Only one application per applicant can be funded.

Important Dates & Deadlines

  • Call Announcement:17 April 2026
  • Deadline to Submit Questions:24 April 2026
  • Webinars for Applicants:
    • 29 April 2026 (09:00–10:00 CET)
    • 30 April 2026 (15:00–16:00 CET)

Note: Webinars will be recorded for those unable to attend live.

Informational Webinars

To support applicants, the IPSN Secretariat will host live sessions to:

  • Explain the application process
  • Clarify eligibility and requirements
  • Answer submitted questions

Participants are encouraged to submit questions in advance via email.

Application Documents

Applicants must review and prepare:

Request for Proposals – Click here

  • Concept note submissionvia the official portal – Click here

All submissions must be made in English.

Why This Grant Matters

This funding opportunity is more than financial support—it is a gateway to:

  • Strengthening global disease surveillance systems
  • Advancing scientific research and innovation
  • Building resilient public health infrastructures
  • Contributing to global health security and pandemic preparedness

Final Thoughts

The IPSN Catalytic Grant Fund 2026/2027 is a unique opportunity for organizations in LMICs to lead transformative change in pathogen surveillance. By supporting cutting-edge research and scalable solutions, this initiative plays a vital role in safeguarding global health.

If your organization is ready to innovate and make a measurable impact, now is the time to act. Learn more about this opportunity on the official WHO website.

Explore More Opportunities for Youth

Want to discover more grant opportunities? Visit the OFY website: Click here

The post Catalytic Grant Fund 2026/2027: Unlocking Innovation in Pathogen Surveillance for Global Health appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
